tavily.answer: In January 2026, Human Rights Monitor reported a significant increase in the number of refugees in Indonesia's West Papua province, reaching 105,878. The Papua Human Rights and Justice Foundation urged President Prabowo to review troop deployment, highlighting ongoing military operations driving new displacement. The Catholic Church in Indonesia called for urgent dialogue and a peaceful resolution as the humanitarian crisis deepened in the region. Attacks by West Papua National Liberation Army rebels pose a threat to development and social stability in Papua. Despite these efforts, there has been no de-escalation in the armed conflict, with military operations continuing to drive new displacement.

acled.note: Your ACLED access tier serves data up to 2025-07-08 (~12-month embargo).

roads.answer: In Papua, Indonesia, there have been reports of conflict leading to road closures, blocked highways, destroyed bridges, checkpoints, and border crossing closures, making evacuation routes impassable. The situation has escalated with ongoing protests and military responses, further complicating movement and access in the region.
